revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=利鴈神社| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市尺戸
大阪府富田林市宮町3丁目2053| 祭神=保食神
品陀別命
天児屋根命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社}} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺戸にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。明治時代に[[富田林市]]宮町の[[美具久留御魂神社]]に[[神社合祀|合祀]]され同社の[[摂社]]となったが、旧社地の尺戸にも同名社が再建された。 ==祭神== [[保食神]]・[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では[[饒速日命]]の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、喜志村字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺戸の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040201-01.html 利鴈神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/tokari.htm 利鴈神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市]]

天児屋根命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社}} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺度にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。明治時代に[[富田林市]]宮町の[[美具久留御魂神社]]に[[神社合祀|合祀]]され同社の[[摂社]]となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 ==祭神== [[保食神]]・[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、[[喜志村]]字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tokari Shrine'''|利雁神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=利鴈神社 | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺度
（大阪府富田林市宮町3丁目2053） | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[保食神]]
[[品陀別命]]
[[天児屋根命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[村社]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺度にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。明治時代に[[富田林市]]宮町の[[美具久留御魂神社]]に[[神社合祀|合祀]]され同社の[[摂社]]となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 == 祭神 == [[保食神]]・[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、[[喜志村]]字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]]

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|利雁神社|version=107260250|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称=利鴈神社 | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺度
（大阪府富田林市宮町3丁目2053） | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[保食神]]
[[品陀別命]]
[[天児屋根命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[村社]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺度にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。明治時代に[[富田林市]]宮町の[[美具久留御魂神社]]に[[神社合祀|合祀]]され同社の[[摂社]]となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 == 祭神 == [[保食神]]・[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、[[喜志村]]字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Q brackets]] {{commons category|Category:Tokari Shrine}} [[JA:利雁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11396948}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|利雁神社|version=107260250|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称=利鴈神社 | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺度
（大阪府富田林市宮町3丁目2053） | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[保食神]]
[[品陀別命]]
[[天児屋根命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[村社]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺度にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。明治時代に[[富田林市]]宮町の[[美具久留御魂神社]]に[[神社合祀|合祀]]され同社の[[摂社]]となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 == 祭神 == [[保食神]]・[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、[[喜志村]]字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

[[天児屋根命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[村社]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]尺度にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。明治時代に[[富田林市]]宮町の[[美具久留御魂神社]]に[[神社合祀|合祀]]され同社の[[摂社]]となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 == 祭神 == [[保食神]]・[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（[[1872年]]）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、[[喜志村]]字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]]

{{jalink|天児屋根命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|村社}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}尺度にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社}}。明治時代に{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}宮町の{{jalink|美具久留御魂神社|Q11608777}}に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}され同社の{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 == 祭神 == {{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|品陀別命}}・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、{{jalink|喜志村|Q18700084}}字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]]

{{jalink|天児屋根命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}尺度にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。明治時代に{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}宮町の{{jalink|美具久留御魂神社|Q11608777}}に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}され同社の{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|品陀別命}}・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 === 歴史 === 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、{{jalink|喜志村|Q18700084}}字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]]

link|Q11396948}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=利鴈神社 | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}尺度
（大阪府富田林市宮町3丁目2053） | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}
{{jalink|品陀別命}}
{{jalink|天児屋根命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}尺度にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。明治時代に{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}宮町の{{jalink|美具久留御魂神社|Q11608777}}に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}され同社の{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|品陀別命}}・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 === 歴史 === 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、{{jalink|喜志村|Q18700084}}字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]

link|Q11396948}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=利鴈神社 | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}尺度
（大阪府富田林市宮町3丁目2053） | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}
{{jalink|品陀別命}}
{{jalink|天児屋根命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}尺度にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。明治時代に{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}宮町の{{jalink|美具久留御魂神社|Q11608777}}に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}され同社の{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|品陀別命}}・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 === 歴史 === 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、{{jalink|喜志村|Q18700084}}字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}

{{jalink|天児屋根命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=利鴈神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''利鴈神社'''（とかりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}尺度にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。明治時代に{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}宮町の{{jalink|美具久留御魂神社|Q11608777}}に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}され同社の{{jalink|摂社|Q135009904}}となったが、旧社地の尺度にも同名社が再建された。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}・{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|品陀別命}}・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}を祭神とする。 『大日本史神祇志』や『地理志料』では{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の末裔の依羅氏の祖神を祀ると記している。『神社要録』では「祭神不詳」としている。 === 歴史 === 創建の由緒は不詳である。元は尺土村の西南の利雁山に鎮座し「王の宮」とも称していたという。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では小社に列している。天正年間の兵乱で社殿を失い、慶長17年に遷座した。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に村社に列格したが、明治40年10月12日、{{jalink|喜志村|Q18700084}}字宮（現 富田林市宮町）の美具久留御魂神社に合祀された。 再建時期は不詳であるが、合祀後、羽曳野市尺度の西方寺の西側に利雁神社が再建された。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}

revision.comment: AI-assisted translation: Japanese wiki content translated by local AI agents (deepseek-r1:8b via Ollama, reviewed/assembled by Claude Code). Quality varies — some pages may contain subtle translation errors.

Tokarishine.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180885 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|lt=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == native label (P1705) == * {'text': '利雁神社', 'language': 'ja-hani'} ** P1814: とかりじんじゃ ** P2125: Tokari-jinja == Commons category (P373) == * Tokari Shrine == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039108|lt=Q135039108|WD=Q135039108}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市尺度 大阪府富田林市宮町3丁目2053', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府富田林市宮町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tokari Shrine}} [[JA:利雁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11396948}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}
{{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) / former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shakudo, {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. In the Meiji period it was {{jalink|shrine merger|Q11590700|merged}} into {{jalink|Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777}} in Miya-chō, {{jalink|Tondabayashi|Q490928}}, becoming a {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}, {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{jalink|Kishi Village|Q18700084}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}
{{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) / former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shakudo, {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. In the Meiji period it was {{jalink|shrine merger|Q11590700|merged}} into {{jalink|Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777}} in Miya-chō, {{jalink|Tondabayashi|Q490928}}, becoming a {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}, {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{jalink|Kishi Village|Q18700084}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}

The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}

link|Q11396948}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}
{{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) / former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shakudo, {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. In the Meiji period it was {{jalink|shrine merger|Q11590700|merged}} into {{jalink|Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777}} in Miya-chō, {{jalink|Tondabayashi|Q490928}}, becoming a {{jalink|sessha|Q135009904}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}, {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{jalink|Kishi Village|Q18700084}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}[[Category:qq]]

link|Q11396948}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}
{{ill||lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine}} [[JA:利雁神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11396948}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084|1=Kishi}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084|1=Kishi}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とかりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)}}[[Category:qq]]

{{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084|1=Kishi}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]]

{{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084|1=Kishi}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]]

{{神社 | 名称=Tokari Shrine | 画像= [[File:Tokarishine.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084|1=Kishi}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]]

Torii and worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Shakudo
(3-2053 Miya-chō, Tondabayashi, Osaka) | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084|1=Kishi}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tokari Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tokari Shrine''' (とかりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shakudo, {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084|1=Kishi}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]]

In the Meiji period it was {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into {{ill|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|Q11608777|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} in Miya-chō, {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, becoming a {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} of that shrine, but a shrine of the same name was later rebuilt at the old site in Shakudo. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. ''Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi'' and ''Chiri Shiryo'' state that it enshrines the ancestral deity of the Yora clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' lists "deity unknown." === History === The shrine's founding history is unknown. It originally stood on Mount Tokari in the southwest of Shakudo village and was also called "Ō no Miya." In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a minor shrine. The shrine buildings were lost in warfare in the Tenshō era, and it was relocated in Keichō 17. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, but on October 12, Meiji 40, it was merged into Migukurumitama Shrine in Aza Miya, {{ill|lt=Kishi Village|Q18700084|WD=Q18700084|1=Kishi}} (present-day Miya-chō, Tondabayashi). The time of rebuilding is unknown, but after the merger Tokari Shrine was rebuilt west of Saifuku-ji in Shakudo, Habikino. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tokari Shrine (Q11396948)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Migukurumitama Shrine]] [[Category:Keidai-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]]
